title: The Great Digital Exodus
subtitle: How America's TikTok ban triggered a global chain reaction ending in expensive burritos

ripples.headline: Instagram Reels servers crash as 170 million users migrate simultaneously
Chinese tech stocks plummet 15% amid fears of broader US tech decoupling
European Union fast-tracks digital sovereignty legislation citing US precedent
YouTube advertising rates surge 40% as brands scramble for short-form video inventory
Influencer marketing agencies report 60% revenue drop as creator economy fragments
Food delivery apps overwhelmed as former TikTok food reviewers flood platforms
Local burrito shop raises prices 20% due to sudden influx of food bloggers and delayed avocado shipments

symphony_arc.opening: The digital earthquake begins at midnight EST as 170 million Americans lose their primary social platform
symphony_arc.development: Cascading server failures and market panic spread globally as the tech ecosystem scrambles to adapt
symphony_arc.climax: Geopolitical tensions peak as nations reconsider their digital dependencies and sovereignty
symphony_arc.resolution: The dust settles into a new normal where even local businesses feel the ripples of digital disruption
